description_long: This is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with three broad, flat blades radiating from a central hub. The blades are evenly spaced and have a slightly rounded rectangular shape, each attached to a robust, circular motor housing. The fan is suspended from a straight, cylindrical downrod that connects to a small mounting canopy at the top, which would attach to a ceiling. The entire structure is finished in a uniform reddish-brown color, giving it a cohesive and utilitarian appearance. There is no visible blade guard, exposing the blades fully for maximum airflow. No power cord or control panel is visible, suggesting the controls are likely integrated into the wall or ceiling wiring.
description: A reddish-brown ceiling-mounted electric fan with three wide, flat blades and a simple, exposed design. The fan features a cylindrical downrod and lacks a visible blade guard or control panel.
description_view_0: The fan is seen from a slightly angled perspective, showing all three blades and the central hub clearly.
description_view_1: This view highlights the length of the blades and the cylindrical downrod, with the blades appearing more foreshortened.
description_view_2: The fan is viewed from the opposite side, with the central hub and the attachment point to the ceiling more visible.
description_view_3: This angle shows the fan from another side, emphasizing the symmetry and spacing of the blades.
attribute_blade_guard_design: There is no blade guard present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective covering.
